name: Olive (food medium)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 169094 (Olives, ripe, canned; SR Legacy NDB 09193)
components.evidence: USDA canned ripe olives 79.99 g; oil-rich fresh mesocarp is lower (~50-70%); reported ~75 g as a fresh-fruit typical.

components.name: total lipid (olive oil / triacylglycerol)
components.evidence: USDA canned ripe = 10.68 g; fresh oil-rich mesocarp commonly 15-30%. Oil (triacylglycerols, mostly oleate) is the DOMINANT carbon/energy source of the fruit.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: USDA MUFA total 7.888 g; oleic is the dominant fatty acid of olive oil, IOC range 55-85% of total FA (typ ~70%).

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.evidence: USDA saturated total 1.415 g; palmitic is the main SFA, IOC 7-20% of total FA.

components.name: linoleic acid (C18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: USDA PUFA total 0.911 g; linoleic dominates PUFA, IOC 2.5-21% of total FA.

components.name: stearic acid (C18:0)
components.evidence: USDA 09193 fatty-acid breakdown; IOC 0.5-5% of total FA.

components.name: palmitoleic acid (C16:1 n-7)
components.evidence: IOC 0.3-3.5% of total FA; USDA 09193 lists 16:1.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: IOC <1% of total FA; USDA 09193 lists 18:3.

components.name: gadoleic/gondoic acid (C20:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Trace MUFA in olive oil, IOC <0.5% of total FA.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Marsilio et al. 2001 Food Chem 72:485 doi:10.1016/S0308-8146(00)00268-5; MDPI Agriculture 2022 12(9):1347
components.evidence: Glucose is the principal free sugar of olive pulp; wild/table cultivars 1.0-5.3 g/100g fresh; ~2.5 g typical. MISSING from prior FooDB profile.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Second free sugar; 0.76-5.94 g/100g across cultivars; ~1.5 g typical. MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: D-mannitol
components.evidence: Characteristic fermentable polyol of Olea europaea, 0.43-0.93 g/100g; a growth-relevant carbon source. MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor in olive pulp, 0.15-2.35 g/100g; present in prior profile, now quantified.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Marsilio et al. 2001; review of olive pulp sugars
components.evidence: Reported present in olive pulp at low levels; not reliably quantified.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Nergiz & Ergunel 2010 Czech J Food Sci 28(3):202; review (total pulp acidity 4-10 g/kg as citric)
components.evidence: Most abundant organic acid of olive fruit; green table olives 24.7-188 mg/100g. Dominant non-lipid acid; MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Nergiz & Ergunel 2010 Czech J Food Sci 28(3):202
components.evidence: Second organic acid of olive pulp, rises again at full ripeness. MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Nergiz & Ergunel 2010 Czech J Food Sci 28(3):202
components.evidence: Minor organic acid quantified by HPLC in olive fruit. MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid; 10-30 mg/100g in table olives. MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: Not in fresh fruit; a major product of brine/lactic fermentation in processed table olives. Include (null) only if the fermented form is modelled.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein; amino acids below scale with this pool (each <0.05 g/100g).

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in the 0.84 g protein pool; USDA lists it. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Second most abundant; USDA-listed. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: USDA 09193 amino-acid profile; trace (<0.05 g). Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: glycine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: USDA-listed; also an osmolyte. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid (was absent from prior list; added).

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid (added; also aromatic precursor).

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: USDA-listed; precursor of olive hydroxytyrosol/tyrosol. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: USDA-listed trace amino acid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA lists L-serine (prior profile had D-serine, a non-plant enantiomer; corrected to L).

components.name: choline
components.evidence: USDA 09193 total choline. Present in prior profile.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine compatible solute of Olea europaea. Present in prior profile; not quantified.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: USDA 09193; olive oil is tocopherol-rich. Present in prior profile.

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.evidence: Minor tocopherol; USDA-listed. Present in prior profile.

components.name: vitamin A (as beta-carotene)
components.evidence: beta-carotene 237 ug (RAE 20 ug). Provitamin-A carotenoid; olive is a plant so vitamin A is carotenoid-derived, not retinol.

components.name: beta-cryptoxanthin
components.evidence: USDA 09193 carotenoid. Present in prior profile.

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Dominant carotenoid of olive fruit; USDA 09193 (added).

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile (phytomenadione).

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Low in processed olive; USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile (nicotinate).

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: vitamin B6 (pyridoxine)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Trace/near-zero in USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: USDA 09193 reports ~0 in processed olive; fresh fruit contains low folate. Present in prior profile.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: HIGH due to brining of canned/table olives; fresh olive Na is low (<50 mg). Present in prior profile.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: USDA 09193 (leached during processing; fresh fruit higher). Present in prior profile.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: USDA 09193 (elevated by ferrous-gluconate darkening of ripe olives). Present in prior profile.

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: USDA 09193. Present in prior profile.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion to brine sodium in table olives. Present in prior profile; not separately quantified.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; olive is a plant and contains provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene/cryptoxanthin), not retinol. USDA 09193 retinol = 0. Keep beta-carotene instead.

remove.name: 3-4-Dihydroxyphenylacetate
remove.reason: DOPAC, a mammalian dopamine catabolite; FooDB pathway-predicted, not a measured olive constituent (genuine olive catechols are hydroxytyrosol/DHPEA compounds, kept as uncovered).

remove.name: 3-Methoxy-4-hydroxyphenylacetic acid
remove.reason: Homovanillic acid, a mammalian dopamine metabolite; FooDB-predicted, biologically implausible as an olive nutrient.

remove.name: Homoveratric acid
remove.reason: 3,4-dimethoxyphenylacetic acid, a mammalian catecholamine metabolite; FooDB-predicted, implausible in olive fruit.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: D-enantiomer; plant/food protein amino acids are L-forms. Replaced with L-serine (USDA).

missing_added: citric acid (0.15 g/100g, dominant organic acid)
total olive oil / triacylglycerol quantified (~15 g/100g, dominant carbon source)
keep_uncovered: Oleuropein (defining bitter secoiridoid glucoside, up to ~14% DW in unripe fruit)
sources: USDA FooDData Central FDC 169094 (Olives, ripe, canned, small-extra large; SR Legacy NDB 09193)
Marsilio V. et al. 2001, Food Chemistry 72(4):485-490, doi:10.1016/S0308-8146(00)00268-5 (sugars & polyols of olive fruit)
MDPI Agriculture 2022, 12(9):1347 (ripening effect on sugars & phenolics, cv. Leccino)
Nergiz C. & Ergunel P.G. 2010, Czech J. Food Sci. 28(3):202-205, doi:10.17221/1379-CJFS (organic acids in olive fruit by HPLC)
International Olive Council COI/T.15/NC No 3/Rev.16 (2021) (olive-oil fatty-acid ranges)
Review: total olive-pulp acidity 4-10 g/kg expressed as citric acid
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources of olive are (1) OLIVE OIL / triacylglycerols, overwhelmingly oleate, and (2) the free sugars + mannitol of the pulp; organic acids (citric>malic) are the main previously-missing metabolites. Prior FooDB profile lacked all free sugars, mannitol and organic acids and under-specified the oil. Micronutrient/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel is from USDA canned ripe olives (09193); note that canning/brining elevates Na and Fe and leaches sugars/K, so sugar and organic-acid amounts here are taken from fresh-fruit literature, not the processed USDA record. Amino acids are present only at trace levels (total protein 0.84 g/100g) and are left with null amounts. Phenolic secoiridoids (oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol) define the fruit chemically but have no BiGG metabolite, so they remain in keep_uncovered.
